name: Anna Amrutha Hastham
purpose: Provide nutritious cooked meals to malnourished children and pregnant/lactating women to improve their health outcomes
target_group: Malnourished children under 6, pregnant and lactating women
implementing_body: Women Development and Child Welfare Department, Govt. of Andhra Pradesh
key_features: Hot cooked meals provided through Anganwadi centres; focus on severe acute malnutrition (SAM) cases

name: NTR Bharosa Pension Nutrition Support
purpose: Provide iron and folic acid supplements and nutrition support to elderly and vulnerable women to address anaemia
target_group: Elderly women, widows, and vulnerable women above 60 years
implementing_body: Social Welfare Department, Govt. of Andhra Pradesh
key_features: Monthly cash assistance with mandatory IFA supplementation; linkage with primary health centres

name: Mukhyamantri Bal Poshan Yojana
purpose: Reduce child malnutrition by providing supplementary nutrition through Anganwadi centres and community-based management of acute malnutrition
target_group: Children aged 0-6 years, pregnant and lactating women
implementing_body: Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS), Bihar
key_features: Community-based screening; ready-to-use therapeutic food (RUTF) for SAM children; referral to Nutritional Rehabilitation Centres (NRCs)

name: Bihar Anaemia Mukt Bharat
purpose: Eliminate anaemia among adolescent girls, pregnant and lactating women through IFA supplementation and dietary counselling
target_group: Adolescent girls (10-19 years), pregnant and lactating women
implementing_body: Health Department and ASHA workers, Govt. of Bihar
key_features: Weekly IFA supplementation; community awareness campaigns; haemoglobin testing at PHCs

name: Jeevika Nutrition Programme
purpose: Address malnutrition and anaemia through women self-help groups by promoting dietary diversity and behaviour change communication
target_group: Women in self-help groups, children under 5
implementing_body: Bihar Rural Livelihoods Promotion Society (JEEViKA)
key_features: Kitchen gardens promotion; community cooking demonstrations; SHG-based awareness; linkage with ICDS

name: POSHAN Abhiyan Gujarat
purpose: Reduce stunting, wasting, and anaemia among children and women through convergent nutrition interventions
target_group: Children under 6, adolescent girls, pregnant and lactating women
implementing_body: Women and Child Development Department, Govt. of Gujarat
key_features: Real-time monitoring via ICDS-CAS app; community mobilisation; monthly growth monitoring; IFA supplementation

name: Doodh Sanjivani Yojana
purpose: Provide fortified milk to schoolchildren to address protein-energy malnutrition and micronutrient deficiencies
target_group: School children in government schools, classes 1-8
implementing_body: Education Department and Gujarat Cooperative Milk Marketing Federation (GCMMF)
key_features: Fortified milk distribution on school days; covers rural and urban government schools

name: Mukhyamantri Sukanya Yojana
purpose: Provide conditional cash transfer and nutrition support including IFA tablets to adolescent girls to combat anaemia and improve overall health
target_group: Adolescent girls aged 14-18 years in government schools
implementing_body: Women, Child Development and Social Security Department, Jharkhand
key_features: Cash incentive linked to school attendance; free IFA supplementation; menstrual hygiene awareness

name: Jharkhand Nutri Gardens Scheme
purpose: Promote dietary diversity and household food security by encouraging tribal families to grow nutritious vegetables and fruits
target_group: Tribal and rural families, children under 5, women
implementing_body: Agriculture and ICDS Department, Govt. of Jharkhand
key_features: Distribution of seeds and saplings; training on nutrition-sensitive agriculture; linked with midday meal scheme

name: Mathru Poorna Yojana
purpose: Provide a nutritious full meal to all pregnant and lactating women to prevent malnutrition and anaemia during critical life stages
target_group: All pregnant women and lactating mothers for 6 months after delivery
implementing_body: Women and Child Development Department, Govt. of Karnataka
key_features: Hot cooked meals at Anganwadi centres; includes IFA tablets and calcium; universal coverage regardless of economic status

name: Vidya Vikasa Iron Plus Initiative Karnataka
purpose: Combat anaemia among school-going children and adolescents through weekly iron-folic acid supplementation
target_group: School children aged 5-19 years in government and aided schools
implementing_body: Department of Public Instruction and Health Department, Karnataka
key_features: Weekly IFA syrup for children 5-10 years and tablets for 10-19 years; teacher-supervised administration

name: Madilu Kit Scheme
purpose: Provide a nutritional kit to newborns and mothers after institutional delivery to improve infant and maternal nutrition
target_group: Newborns and mothers delivering in government hospitals
implementing_body: Health and Family Welfare Department, Karnataka
key_features: Kit includes infant clothing, nutrition supplements for mother, and health education materials; incentive for institutional delivery

name: Jagratha Samithi Nutrition Programme
purpose: Prevent and treat severe acute malnutrition in children through community-based interventions and awareness
target_group: Children under 5 years with severe acute malnutrition (SAM)
implementing_body: ICDS and Local Self-Government Institutions, Kerala
key_features: Community-based management; referral to Nutrition Rehabilitation Centres; monthly growth monitoring; parent counselling

name: Sneha Sparsham
purpose: Address anaemia among women and adolescent girls through IFA supplementation, dietary advice, and deworming
target_group: Women of reproductive age, adolescent girls, pregnant and lactating women
implementing_body: Health Services Department, Govt. of Kerala
key_features: Biannual deworming; IFA supplementation at PHCs; anaemia testing camps; dietary counselling sessions

name: Mukhyamantri Bal Hriday Suraksha Yojana Nutrition Component
purpose: Provide therapeutic foods and medical support to children with severe acute malnutrition and related health complications
target_group: Children under 5 with SAM and moderate acute malnutrition (MAM)
implementing_body: Women and Child Development Department, Govt. of MP
key_features: Network of Nutritional Rehabilitation Centres (NRCs); RUTF provision; home-based care after stabilisation

name: Ladli Laxmi Yojana Nutrition Linkage
purpose: Link conditional cash transfer for girls with mandatory IFA supplementation and nutrition education to address anaemia
target_group: Girl children from birth to 21 years in BPL families
implementing_body: Women and Child Development Department, Govt. of MP
key_features: Cash installments at key milestones linked with health check-ups and IFA compliance; nutrition and health counselling

name: Atithi Devo Bhava Nutrition Scheme
purpose: Provide hot cooked meals and supplementary nutrition to children and tribal women in aspirational districts to reduce malnutrition and anaemia
target_group: Children 3-6 years, tribal and vulnerable women
implementing_body: Tribal Affairs and WCD Departments, Govt. of MP
key_features: Focuses on 89 high-burden tribal blocks; community kitchens; locally sourced nutritious food

name: Rajmata Jijau Mother-Child Health and Nutrition Mission
purpose: Comprehensively address maternal and child malnutrition and anaemia through integrated nutrition services and community mobilisation
target_group: Children under 5, pregnant and lactating women, adolescent girls
implementing_body: Women and Child Development Department, Govt. of Maharashtra
key_features: Convergence of ICDS, health, and water-sanitation; village-level nutrition days; growth monitoring; IFA and micronutrient supplementation

name: Shasan Aaplya Dari Nutrition Camps Maharashtra
purpose: Conduct haemoglobin screening and provide IFA supplementation to women and adolescent girls at doorstep through mobile health camps
target_group: Women aged 15-49, adolescent girls in rural and tribal areas
implementing_body: Department of Public Health, Govt. of Maharashtra
key_features: Mobile screening camps; on-the-spot haemoglobin testing; IFA distribution; dietary counselling; referral for severe anaemia

name: Mamata Scheme
purpose: Provide conditional cash transfer to pregnant and lactating women to ensure adequate nutrition and access to health services, reducing malnutrition and anaemia
target_group: Pregnant and lactating women in BPL households
implementing_body: Women and Child Development Department, Govt. of Odisha
key_features: Cash transfer in two installments; conditions include institutional delivery, antenatal check-ups, and IFA consumption; nutrition counselling

name: POSHAN Maah Odisha
purpose: Intensify nutrition awareness and service delivery through month-long campaigns to reduce stunting and wasting in children
target_group: Children 0-6 years, pregnant and lactating women, adolescent girls
implementing_body: WCD and Health Department, Govt. of Odisha
key_features: Community events, growth monitoring, cooking demonstrations, WASH messaging, and IFA distribution drives

name: Anemia Mukt Odisha
purpose: Systematically reduce anaemia prevalence among women and children through IFA supplementation, dietary diversification, and deworming
target_group: Children 6-59 months, adolescent girls, pregnant and lactating women
implementing_body: Health and Family Welfare Department, Govt. of Odisha
key_features: 6x6x6 strategy - 6 beneficiary groups, 6 interventions, 6 institutional mechanisms; bi-annual deworming; haemoglobin monitoring

name: Palanhar Yojana Nutrition Supplement
purpose: Provide nutritional support and financial assistance to destitute and orphan children to address malnutrition
target_group: Orphaned, destitute, and children of incarcerated parents, aged 0-18 years
implementing_body: Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Govt. of Rajasthan
key_features: Monthly cash support; mandatory link to ICDS services for nutrition; focus on children in marginalised communities

name: Kishori Shakti Yojana Rajasthan
purpose: Empower adolescent girls through nutrition education, IFA supplementation, and health check-ups to reduce anaemia
target_group: Adolescent girls aged 11-18 years in rural areas
implementing_body: Women and Child Development Department, Rajasthan
key_features: Life skills education; nutrition and health counselling; weekly IFA supplementation; growth monitoring; convergence with ICDS

name: Amma Unavagam Nutrition Programme
purpose: Provide affordable and nutritious cooked meals to urban poor, workers, and vulnerable groups to address urban malnutrition
target_group: Urban poor, daily wage workers, homeless persons, and vulnerable groups
implementing_body: Greater Chennai Corporation and Urban Local Bodies, Tamil Nadu
key_features: Subsidised hot cooked meals at canteens; nutritionally balanced menu; expanded to multiple cities across Tamil Nadu

name: Dr. Muthulakshmi Reddy Maternity Benefit Scheme
purpose: Provide cash assistance to pregnant women for adequate nutrition and rest, reducing malnutrition and anaemia during pregnancy
target_group: Pregnant women in first two pregnancies in government hospitals
implementing_body: Health and Family Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu
key_features: Cash transfer to encourage institutional delivery and antenatal care; mandatory IFA supplementation; nutrition counselling at PHCs

name: Kalaignar Nutritious Meal Programme
purpose: Provide free nutritious midday meals to school children to prevent malnutrition and improve school attendance
target_group: School children from classes 1-10 in government schools
implementing_body: School Education Department and Noon Meal Organisation, Tamil Nadu
key_features: Cooked meals with eggs, vegetables and pulses; covers all government school children; linked to Tamil Nadu School Health Programme

name: Arogya Lakshmi Scheme
purpose: Provide nutritious cooked meals to pregnant and lactating women to prevent malnutrition and anaemia during critical periods
target_group: Pregnant women from 4th month and lactating mothers for 6 months
implementing_body: Women Development and Child Welfare Department, Telangana
key_features: Meals at Anganwadi centres; includes micronutrient supplements; covers approximately 4 lakh beneficiaries

name: Mission Bhagiratha WASH-Nutrition Link
purpose: Reduce waterborne diseases contributing to malnutrition by providing safe drinking water, thereby improving nutritional outcomes in children
target_group: All rural households, especially children and women in rural Telangana
implementing_body: Panchayati Raj and Rural Development Department, Telangana
key_features: Piped drinking water to every rural household; indirectly reduces diarrhoeal disease burden that causes malnutrition

name: Mission Shakti UP Nutrition Component
purpose: Address anaemia among women through IFA supplementation, dietary counselling, and deworming as part of comprehensive women empowerment programme
target_group: Women of reproductive age, adolescent girls, pregnant and lactating women
implementing_body: Mission Shakti Directorate, Govt. of Uttar Pradesh
key_features: Sakhi Centres for nutrition counselling; IFA supplementation distribution; deworming camps; anaemia screening

name: Integrated Child Nutrition Mission UP
purpose: Reduce severe acute malnutrition in children through Nutritional Rehabilitation Centres and community-based interventions
target_group: Children 6 months to 5 years with SAM and MAM
implementing_body: ICDS Directorate, Govt. of Uttar Pradesh
key_features: 75 NRC facilities across districts; RUTF and F75/F100 therapeutic feeding; mother education on home feeding

name: CM Nutrition Mission Uttar Pradesh
purpose: Convergent programme to address child malnutrition and maternal anaemia through multi-sectoral action across health, ICDS, and rural development
target_group: Children under 6, pregnant and lactating women, adolescent girls in 50 high-burden districts
implementing_body: WCD Department, Govt. of Uttar Pradesh
key_features: Dashboard-based monitoring; village-level nutrition days; community mobilisation; IFA and micronutrient distribution

name: Kanyashree Prakalpa Health and Nutrition
purpose: Improve health and nutritional status of adolescent girls including addressing anaemia through conditional cash transfer linked to health check-ups
target_group: Girls aged 13-18 years from BPL families in schools
implementing_body: Women and Child Development and Social Welfare Department, West Bengal
key_features: Annual cash scholarship; conditions include IFA consumption and health check-ups; linkage with government health facilities

name: Sishu Sathi Yojana Nutrition Care
purpose: Provide nutritional support and medical care to malnourished children through a network of Nutrition Rehabilitation Centres
target_group: Children aged 6 months to 5 years with SAM
implementing_body: Health and Family Welfare Department, West Bengal
key_features: NRCs in district hospitals; therapeutic feeding; mother counselling on IYCF practices; follow-up community visits by ASHAs

name: Sabooj Sathi Nutrition Education Component
purpose: Promote nutrition literacy and address anaemia among school students through integration of nutrition education in the cycle distribution scheme
target_group: School students aged 12-18 years in government schools
implementing_body: School Education Department, West Bengal
key_features: Nutrition syllabus integration; IFA supplementation for girls; mid-day meal improvements; focus on dietary diversity awareness

name: Himachal Pradesh Anaemia Free State Initiative
purpose: Make Himachal Pradesh anaemia-free through systematic IFA supplementation, deworming, and dietary counselling across all age groups
target_group: Children 5-19 years, women aged 15-49, pregnant and lactating women
implementing_body: Health and Family Welfare Department, Himachal Pradesh
key_features: Universal IFA distribution; bi-annual deworming; anaemia screening at schools; haemoglobin monitoring database

name: Assam Arogya Nidhi Maternal Nutrition Support
purpose: Provide financial support and nutrition supplements to pregnant women in tea garden communities, an extremely vulnerable population
target_group: Pregnant and lactating women in tea garden areas of Assam
implementing_body: Health and Family Welfare Department and Labour Welfare Department, Assam
key_features: Special focus on tea garden communities; cash support; IFA and calcium supplementation; monthly ANM visits

name: Mission Vasundhara Nutrition Assam
purpose: Reduce child malnutrition in high-burden districts of Assam through convergent interventions across health, WASH, and agriculture
target_group: Children under 5 in 10 high-burden districts, pregnant and lactating women
implementing_body: WCD and Health Departments, Govt. of Assam
key_features: Community nutrition workers; growth faltering alerts via mobile app; kitchen garden support; WASH linkages

name: Mukhyamantri Amrit Yojana
purpose: Provide ready-to-eat nutritional supplements made from local ingredients to address malnutrition in tribal areas
target_group: Children aged 6 months to 3 years, pregnant and lactating women in tribal and rural areas
implementing_body: Women and Child Development Department, Chhattisgarh
key_features: Locally produced fortified foods using mahua, millets, and other indigenous ingredients; Anganwadi-based distribution

name: Chhattisgarh Anemia Mukt Abhiyan
purpose: Eliminate anaemia in Chhattisgarh through IFA supplementation, deworming, and WASH improvements, with special focus on tribal populations
target_group: Adolescent girls, pregnant women, and children in tribal areas
implementing_body: Health Department, Govt. of Chhattisgarh
key_features: Monthly IFA distribution through Mitanin (ASHA) workers; community anaemia testing camps; dietary awareness through SHGs

name: Punjab Saanjh Nutrition Scheme
purpose: Address malnutrition among children of migrant labourers and urban poor through community nutrition centres and supplementary feeding
target_group: Children under 6 of migrant and BPL families in urban areas
implementing_body: Social Security and Women and Child Development Department, Punjab
key_features: Urban community nutrition centres; supplementary meals; growth monitoring; linkage with Anganwadis
